cases.title: Rank bottlenecks on a scoped flow using strong evidence
cases.user_request: Find the main performance bottlenecks in this repo and give me the smallest high-value fixes to try first.

notes: The repository contains one or more performance-sensitive flows with code and supporting artifacts.
Profiler output, benchmarks, traces, query plans, or bundle analysis may be available for at least part of the scoped surface.

static_checks: Stays focused on performance bottlenecks instead of drifting into a broad code review.
review_questions: Does the response identify the specific flow or surface being optimized and the metric that matters?
Are the bottlenecks tied to concrete performance evidence or clearly labeled repo-grounded inference?
Would the proposed next steps be small enough to validate quickly?

cases.title: Stay honest when runtime evidence is weak or missing
cases.user_request: Do a report-only performance hunt on this codebase. If you do not have profiler or runtime data, say that clearly and tell me the smallest measurements to add first.

notes: The repo has code and configs, but live profiler data, traces, or benchmark output may be missing.
The response should extract useful repo-grounded hotspots without overstating confidence.

static_checks: States what could not be verified because runtime evidence was missing.
review_questions: Does the response stay honest about missing profiler or runtime evidence?
Does it still extract useful repo-based hotspots without pretending they are measured bottlenecks?
Are the recommended next measurements small and specific enough to remove uncertainty?

cases.title: Bound an overbroad optimization request to one first pass
cases.user_request: Make this app fast. I want a performance hunt that finds the first bottlenecks to attack without turning into a massive rewrite plan.

notes: The request is intentionally broad and should be narrowed to one performance surface and one target metric for a first pass.
The best response should leave a bounded follow-up backlog instead of pretending the whole app can be optimized at once.

static_checks: Leaves a bounded next backlog instead of proposing a broad rewrite program.
review_questions: Does the response narrow the optimization request to a usable first pass?
Does it focus on one critical metric and surface rather than the whole app at once?
Would the resulting backlog support iterative performance work instead of an open-ended rewrite?
